title: Intensely Buttery Norwegian Pasta Casserole
description: A rich and comforting Norwegian dish featuring bowtie pasta in a buttery, gooey sauce, cooked to perfection in a clay pot. This Buddhist-temple-friendly recipe is perfect for a cozy dinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cook the bowtie pasta according to package instructions until al dente.
In a large saucepan, whisk together the melted butter and flour to make a roux.
Slowly pour in the heavy cream, whisking continuously to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until it thickens.
Remove the sauce from the heat and stir in the grana padano cheese until melted.
In a large clay pot, combine the cooked pasta, cheese sauce, and chopped parsley.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the clay pot to the preheated oven and bake for 20 minutes.
Remove the casserole from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
